findings.title: Required reviewer-lane coverage can be spoofed via reviewer_id substrings
findings.rationale: Clean writeback lane coverage is determined by substring checks on free-form reviewer_id values instead of a trusted lane-role binding. Any actor that can write reviewer outputs can mint two artifacts with crafted reviewer_id strings (for example including both code/bug and spec/intent terms) and satisfy distinct-lane requirements without proving those outputs came from distinct mandated reviewer lanes.
findings.suggested_next_action: Bind reviewer outputs to a canonical lane role (for example enum lane_role persisted by parent orchestration and verified at writeback) and require one artifact per required role, rejecting reviewer_id-only heuristics for cleanliness gating.
